projects.title: Unit 4 Project — Rates & Percents in Action
projects.desc: Capstone: apply percent of a number to a real-world challenge.

projects.title: Percent Shadow-Caster

projects.title: Discount Dash
projects.desc: Arcade game applying percent of a number to find discounts and sale prices.

title: Find the Percent of a Number
contentObjective: I can find the percent of a number using an equation or a model.
languageObjective: I can explain my steps using the words percent, base, part, and equation.

turnAndTalk.question: On Discount Day the claw machine is 25% off its 60-ticket price. In '25% of 60,' which number is the base (the whole), and what part are you trying to find?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The base is 60 tickets and the part is the 25% discount we need to find.

stems.en: The base is ___ and the part I want is ___.
stems.es: La base es ___ y la parte que quiero es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students identify 60 as the base, the 25% discount as the part to find, and connect 'of' to the base.
turnAndTalk.extend: Without computing exactly, will the discount be more or less than half of 60? Use a benchmark to estimate and explain.
extendStems: The discount will be ___ than half because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: What steps did you follow every time to find the percent of a number in the discount table?
turnAndTalk.kernel: I changed the percent to a decimal, then multiplied it by the base to get the part.
turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students describe the equation part = percent x base, converting the percent to a decimal first (e.g., 0.40 x 80 = 32).
turnAndTalk.extend: Compare finding 25% of 60 with the decimal 0.25 versus dividing 60 into 4 equal parts. Why do both methods give the same part?
extendStems: Both give ___ because 25% is the same as ___.

turnAndTalk.question: The arcade donates 12% of Saturday's 1,500 ticket sales. The manager says 180 tickets, a student says 18. Who is right, and how do you know?
turnAndTalk.kernel: 12% of 1,500 is 0.12 times 1,500, which is 180 tickets.

stems.en: The ___ is correct because 12% of 1,500 = ___ x 1,500 = ___.
stems.es: El/La ___ tiene razón porque 12% de 1,500 = ___ x 1,500 = ___.

stems.en: The mistake was using ___ instead of ___.
stems.es: El error fue usar ___ en lugar de ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute 0.12 x 1,500 = 180 and explain the student likely used 0.012 (divided by 1,000) instead of 0.12.
turnAndTalk.extend: Estimate first: 10% of 1,500 is easy to find. Use that to check whether 180 is reasonable for 12%, and explain your reasoning.
extendStems: 10% of 1,500 is ___, so 12% should be a little ___.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Find the Percent of a Number, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: To find the part, change the percent to a decimal, then multiply: part = percent × base.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.RP.3c — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: A way to compare a number to 100, shown with the % sign.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una manera de comparar un número con 100, con el signo %.
vocabulary.cloze: A number out of 100 is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: The whole amount you take a percent of.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La cantidad total de la que sacas un porcentaje.
vocabulary.cloze: In a percent problem, the whole amount you take the percent of is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: The piece you get when you take a percent of a number.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La parte que obtienes al sacar un porcentaje de un número.
vocabulary.cloze: The piece of the whole that the percent represents is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: A math sentence with an equal sign showing both sides are the same.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una oración matemática con un signo igual que muestra que ambos lados son iguales.
vocabulary.cloze: A math sentence with an equal sign, like part = percent × base, is an ___.

vocabulary.definition: Money taken off the first price to make it cheaper.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Dinero que se quita del precio original para que sea más barato.
vocabulary.cloze: An amount subtracted from a price is a ___.

launch.narrative: It's Discount Day at the arcade! Different games are offering percent-off deals on ticket prices. The claw machine is 25% off its usual 60-ticket price, and the racing game is 40% off 80 tickets. You need to calculate the discounts to figure out how many tickets each game actually costs!
launch.contextImage: Arcade games with colorful discount signs showing various percent-off deals
noticePrompts: What information do you need to find the discount amount?
How is finding 25% of 60 different from finding 40% of 80?
wonderPrompts: Is there more than one way to find a percent of a number?
How does a bar model help you see the percent of a number?

conceptIntro.heading: How do I find the percent of a number?
conceptIntro.intro: The base is the whole amount. The part is the piece you get when you take a percent of the base. The word "of" tells you to multiply.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: To find the part, change the percent to a decimal, then multiply: part = percent × base.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: The claw machine is 25% off its 60-ticket price. I want the discount.
First I change 25% to a decimal: 25 ÷ 100 = 0.25.
Then I multiply by the base: 0.25 × 60 = 15.
So the discount is 15 tickets. The part is 15.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Let's find 40% of 80. What is 40% as a decimal? (0.40)

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the table, change each percent to a decimal, then multiply by the base.
Find the discount amount for each arcade game.

explore.instructions: Find the percent of each number. Use the equation: Part = Percent × Base. Convert the percent to a decimal first.

discourse.prompt: What steps do you follow every time you find the percent of a number?
discourse.sentenceFrame: To find ___% of ___, I first convert the percent to ___, then I multiply ___ × ___ = ___.

optional.stem: The claw machine prize is 80 tickets and today it is 25% off. How many tickets do you save?
Your bowling score was 60 points. The bonus round adds 15% more points. How many bonus points is that?
A snack at the arcade costs $40. The sales tax is 5%. How many dollars is the tax?

optional.label: Match each percent-of-a-number tip to its result.

optional.instructions: Each arcade game shows a percent of a number. Sort each one into the correct result bin.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Discount Dash Color-by-Code Error
optional.correctWork: First change 20% to a decimal: 20 ÷ 100 = 0.20. Then multiply by the base: 0.20 × 50 = 10. So 20% of 50 = 10, not 20.
hints: A percent is not a whole number by itself — change 20% to a decimal before you multiply.
20% = 0.20. Then multiply 0.20 × 50 to find the part.

approaching.explanation: 10% = 0.10. Multiply: 0.10 × 320 = 32. (Or move the decimal one place left.)

approaching.instructions: Find each percent of a number using the formula: Part = Percent × Base.

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Find the Percent of a Number.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: To find the part, change the percent to a decimal, then multiply: part = percent × base.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: To find the part, change the percent to a decimal, then multiply: part = percent × base.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: To find the part, change the percent to a decimal, then multiply: part = percent × base.

onLevel.instructions: Complete each row to find the percent of a number.

onLevel.stem: An arcade prize costs 240 tickets. If you have a coupon for 15% off, how many tickets do you save?

onLevel.instructions: A game has 200 total points. Use the bar model to find 25% of 200.
onLevel.label: 200 ÷ 4 = 50. Each 25% section = 50 points. So 25% of 200 = 50.

onLevel.stem: A student scored 72 out of 90 on a test. What percent did they score?

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Find the Percent of a Number.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: To find the part, change the percent to a decimal, then multiply: part = percent × base.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: To find the part, change the percent to a decimal, then multiply: part = percent × base.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: To find the part, change the percent to a decimal, then multiply: part = percent × base.

extending.title: Find the Percent Error
extending.correctWork: 30% = 0.30 (divide by 100, not by 10). 0.30 × 90 = 27. The correct answer is 27.
hints: To convert a percent to a decimal, divide by 100 — how many places does the decimal move?
30% = 0.30, not 3.0. The student divided by 10 instead of 100.

extending.prompt: A class has 32 students. 25% are in the math club, 50% are in the reading club, and 12.5% are in both. How many students are in each group? Can the total of all three groups be more than 32? Explain why or why not.
extending.sentenceFrame: There are ___ in math club, ___ in reading club, and ___ in both. The total ___ be more than 32 because ___.

extending.instructions: A video game character starts with 500 health points. Complete the table to find how many health points are lost or gained.

optionalActivity.name: Discount Dash Color-by-Code
optionalActivity.intro: Power up your arcade by finding the percent of a number at each Discount Dash stage and matching it to the color code!

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Find the Percent of a Number is skipping the key idea: "To find the part, change the percent to a decimal, then multiply: part = percent × base." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: The arcade is donating 12% of Saturday's ticket sales to charity. They sold 1,500 tickets on Saturday. The manager says they will donate 180 tickets. A student says it should be 18 tickets.
connect.promptQuestion: Who is correct, and how do you know?
connect.prompt: The ___ is correct because 12% of 1,500 = ___ × 1,500 = ___. The mistake was ___.
